system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Host: macOS (15.6)
User home directory: /Users/ktoclaw
Current working directory: /Users/ktoclaw/.hermes/hermes-agent

You are running as a scheduled cron job. There is no user present — you cannot ask questions, request clarification, or wait for follow-up. Execute the task fully and autonomously, making reasonable decisions where needed. Your final response is automatically delivered to the job's configured destination — put the primary content directly in your response.

Conversation started: Thursday, May 28, 2026
Model: MiniMax-M2.7
Provider: minimax-oauth

messages.content: Search for Ukraine war news developments in the past 7 days. Return factual, neutral summary with dates and sources.

I'll search for Ukraine war news by checking multiple news sources. Let me fetch recent articles.
<minimax:tool_call>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="description">Fetch BBC Ukraine news RSS</parameter>
<parameter name="command">curl -s --max-time 15 "https://feeds.bbci.co.uk/news/world/europe/rss.xml" 2>/dev/null | head -200</parameter>
</invoke>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="description">Fetch Reuters Ukraine war news</parameter>
<parameter name="command">curl -s --max-time 15 "https://feeds.reuters.com/reuters/world/europe" 2>/dev/null | head -200</parameter>
</invoke>
</minimax:tool_call>
messages.reasoning:
The user wants me to search for Ukraine war news developments in the past 7 days. Since I don't have direct web search tools available, I need to use what I have. Let me check if there are any tools available that might help, or if I should use my training data.

Looking at the available tools, I don't see a web search tool. However, I can try to access news through curl or other means. But since this is a cron job running without user interaction, let me do what I can.

Let me try to fetch news using curl to some news APIs or RSS feeds. I could try:
1. News API
2. RSS feeds from news sources
3. Direct news websites

Let me try to fetch from some news sources to get recent Ukraine war news. I'll try multiple sources to get a comprehensive view.
